issue_title: <Imp Enhancement> Learner username is not displayed on the top right corner of the browser on a Portrait mode in a tablet.
issue_body:
## Summary
Learner username is not displayed on the top right corner of the browser on a Portrait mode in a tablet.

When the tablet is key in landscape mode the learners can see their logged in username on the top right corner, but when you switch it to portrait mode learner's login ID is not visible and on top of that he/she cannot know who's ID he is logged in, as the username is not visible on the dropdown option on the top right corner.

**Important user story**
Its always good to have the learners name displayed all times at the top right corner, so that the learner is aware that he/she is using their own account. This is a big issue since we have't implemented the #2131 and because of this at-least 50% of the leaner end up progressing on the wrong student account. Some learners prefer using Portrait mode and some prefer Landscape mode, hence is enhancement is important.

## System information
 - Version: 0.4.5 and 0.5.0
 - Operating system: Ubuntu 14.04LTS
 - Browser: Chrome.

## How to reproduce
1. Login in a tablet on Landscape mode, notice the user name is clearly visible at the top right corner.
2. Now switch to Portrait mode and notice the username is not visible and not found in the dropdown too.
3. The learner has to got to profiles to know the username.

## Screenshots
On landscape mode:
![img_20170919_162516](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/13453618/30801189-113b35f2-a200-11e7-891e-68111c2b5ea2.jpg)

On Portrait mode:
![img_20170919_162503](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/13453618/30801195-1a3cdd40-a200-11e7-9d88-89d5f498ecb6.jpg)



## Real-life consequences (anything community should be aware of, for instance how it affects your deployment)
Learner's end up using the wrong user accounts most of the time. Consequence is  👎  :)

issue_title: highlight found words to help users understand their results
issue_labels: [{'id': 732420792, 'url': 'https://api.github.com/repos/learningequality/kolibri/labels/TAG:%20needs%20design', 'name': 'TAG: needs design', 'color': 'c1e23b', 'default': False}, {'id': 492816719, 'url': 'https://api.github.com/repos/learningequality/kolibri/labels/TASK:%20ux%20update', 'name': 'TASK: ux update', 'color': '72486f', 'default': False}]
issue_body:
### Observed behavior

If someone searches for "Kung Fu" and an item called "Fun with math" shows up, it might not be immediately obvious that the string "fu" caused the match.

### Expected behavior

Users will better understand search results if the matching text is highlighted, like "[Fu]n with math" (where the stuff in brackets has a bright background color).

We need to be careful to sanitize text because this could require rendering user-inputted text as HTML.

One possible resource: https://community.algolia.com/vue-instantsearch/components/highlight.html

### User-facing consequences

The reason why things show up in search results might not be obvious.

### Context

See #3670

issue_title: Encoding Standard
issue_body:
html5lib has a bunch of [aliases for character encodings](https://github.com/html5lib/html5lib-python/blob/e269a2fd0aafcd83af7cf1e65bba65c0e5a2c18b/html5lib/constants.py#L2851). This should probably be aligned with the [Encoding Standard](http://encoding.spec.whatwg.org/).

I made a Python implementation, hopefully it will help: http://pythonhosted.org/webencodings/ (It mostly does aliases and BOM detection, the actual codec implementations are Python’s.)

Maybe related: #120

issue_title: SecurityRule 'any' attributes inconsistent behavior
issue_body: `policies.SecurityRule` has certain attributes that default to the string value 'any'. However when a SecurityRule is created from a live device and that rule has attributes actually set to 'any' the value is represented as a list `['any']`. This is inconsistent behavior and leads the developer to add extra checks to deal with these attributes when their value is actually set to 'any'.

issue_title: multi tenancy proposal
issue_body:
First I want to state Im not expert in security but Ive tested a thought I had about securing the the data deis stores in etcd .

First an key in Etcd that starts with a underscore (_) can not be listed so if the system were to use a large random string that could be set a install or even better updated that started with a underscore would limit any one on the platform from getting to keys that they should not be able to access . Ive tested this and it dose work so for example I replaced the etcd keys that started with ```deis``` to
   
    _somereallylongrandomstringwithnumbers/database/adminUser

so as I siad Im no security expert but if we could make this key be updated after install so it can be changed to stop long running brut force guessing of the hash string than I think that can secure access the to hidden keys.

It would make sense when I have to start a more complex pod (eg. it uses nfs storage from outside, which can be handled by kubernetes fine not like deis) but I would like that pod to be part of deis system.

issue_title: logspout does not recover on container restart (V1)
issue_body:
I have noticed this behaviour for quite some while now but never bothered to try to debug it.

I noticed that logspout basically stops logging after service restarts (eg deploys or any other reason why new containers are booted)

Note I am on a stateless V1.13.2 cluster with CoreOS 1068.8.0 and applied fix #5074 (although I do not think that matters)
I believe I had similar issues in V1.12 (stateful cluster) but I blamed that on "deis-logspout now continuously re-discovers the logger’s connection information, should it be re-scheduled onto another host" which got released in V1.13 so I had hoped my problems would go away with V1.13.

At this point I can not tell if this is actually a new issue in V1.13; if it is related to staless vs statefull (I don't think it is by looking at the backtraces), or if it is related to newer CoreOS version, or if the problem exists for a long time already


So here is the complete scenario (complete logs: http://pastebin.com/6a6ksEE6 but I recommend you read the scenario first)

* restart logspout so it is in a working state (eg it logs attaching to nodeABC on startup for all containers)
* Restart a container (eg `deis ps:restart`)
* At the moment the old container is destroyed this gets detected; but logspout crashes
~~~
Aug 06 09:32:51 eu1-cloud-worker-01 sh[5391]: 2016/08/06 09:32:51 event: 417ba0517a81 kill
Aug 06 09:32:51 eu1-cloud-worker-01 sh[5391]: 2016/08/06 09:32:51 event: 417ba0517a81 die

Aug 06 09:32:52 eu1-cloud-worker-01 sh[5391]: main.NewAttachManager.func1(0xc82009a180, 0xc820011160)
Aug 06 09:32:52 eu1-cloud-worker-01 sh[5391]:         /home/jenkins/workspace/test-acceptance/src/github.com/deis/deis/logspout/attacher.go:35 +0x3f0
Aug 06 09:32:52 eu1-cloud-worker-01 sh[5391]: created by main.NewAttachManager
Aug 06 09:32:52 eu1-cloud-worker-01 sh[5391]:         /home/jenkins/workspace/test-acceptance/src/github.com/deis/deis/logspout/attacher.go:41 +0x313
~~~
* Logspout crashing is "okay"; it gets restarted automatically and can even attach to containers
~~~
Aug 06 09:32:52 eu1-cloud-worker-01 systemd[1]: deis-logspout.service: Unit entered failed state.
Aug 06 09:32:52 eu1-cloud-worker-01 systemd[1]: deis-logspout.service: Failed with result 'exit-code'.

Aug 06 09:32:58 eu1-cloud-worker-01 sh[7052]: 2016/08/06 09:32:58 attach: 4c745b55f30a bplpp-staging_v109.web.1 success
....
~~~
* Then the new container is started again gets detected by logspout but crashes again in similar way
~~~
Aug 06 09:33:06 eu1-cloud-worker-01 sh[7052]: 2016/08/06 09:33:06 event: 10.133.20.19 pull
Aug 06 09:33:06 eu1-cloud-worker-01 sh[7052]: 2016/08/06 09:33:06 event: 5913ff325cb6 create
Aug 06 09:33:06 eu1-cloud-worker-01 sh[7052]: 2016/08/06 09:33:06 event: 5913ff325cb6 attach
Aug 06 09:33:07 eu1-cloud-worker-01 sh[7052]: panic: runtime error: slice bounds out of range
Aug 06 09:33:07 eu1-cloud-worker-01 sh[7052]: goroutine 79 [running]:
Aug 06 09:33:07 eu1-cloud-worker-01 sh[7052]: main.NewAttachManager.func1(0xc820086180, 0xc820010e80)
Aug 06 09:33:07 eu1-cloud-worker-01 sh[7052]:         /home/jenkins/workspace/test-acceptance/src/github.com/deis/deis/logspout/attacher.go:35 +0x3f0
Aug 06 09:33:07 eu1-cloud-worker-01 sh[7052]: created by main.NewAttachManager
Aug 06 09:33:07 eu1-cloud-worker-01 sh[7052]:         /home/jenkins/workspace/test-acceptance/src/github.com/deis/deis/logspout/attacher.go:41 +0x313
~~~
* Ok so logspout crashes; fleet and/or systemd will handle this for us right?
~~~
Aug 06 09:33:07 eu1-cloud-worker-01 systemd[1]: deis-logspout.service: Main process exited, code=exited, status=2/INVALIDARGUMENT
Aug 06 09:33:07 eu1-cloud-worker-01 docker[7394]: Failed to stop container (deis-logspout): Error response from daemon: No such container: deis-logspout
Aug 06 09:33:07 eu1-cloud-worker-01 systemd[1]: deis-logspout.service: Unit entered failed state.
Aug 06 09:33:07 eu1-cloud-worker-01 systemd[1]: deis-logspout.service: Failed with result 'exit-code'.
Aug 06 09:33:12 eu1-cloud-worker-01 systemd[1]: deis-logspout.service: Service hold-off time over, scheduling restart.
Aug 06 09:33:12 eu1-cloud-worker-01 systemd[1]: Stopped deis-logspout.
Aug 06 09:33:12 eu1-cloud-worker-01 systemd[1]: Starting deis-logspout...
Aug 06 09:33:13 eu1-cloud-worker-01 systemd[1]: Started deis-logspout.
Aug 06 09:33:33 eu1-cloud-worker-01 sh[7579]: 2016/08/06 09:33:33 routing all to udp://10.133.20.195:514
Aug 06 09:33:43 eu1-cloud-worker-01 sh[7579]: 2016/08/06 09:33:43 routing all to udp://10.133.20.195:514
Aug 06 09:33:53 eu1-cloud-worker-01 sh[7579]: 2016/08/06 09:33:53 routing all to udp://10.133.20.195:514
~~~

So it did restart the service, but for some reason it did not attach to ANY containers but silently does nothing.

Note that it still listens for events and the same cycle can be repeated again. It is undeterministic if at the end it will actually have attached to containers or not


So I see 2 probably distinct bugs

A) It crashes on a container restart (this seems to be consistent and we get a stacktrace so probably quite easy to debug and solve). At minimum this will give us some missing logs during container restarts (probably the boot logs of a container which is very frustrating if that container is crashing on boot and there is no easy way to see the logs)

B) It does not seem to attach to containers on startup (this is without logs and does not happen every time, I suspect some sort of race condition in the boot sequence)

If there is anything I can do to help debug this issue (eg try out a custom logspout image) I am obviously willing to do so

issue_title: Let the admin delete an user form the CLI
issue_body:
If I am admin of a deis cluster and there's a normal user which has to be removed, it would be useful to be able to do so from the command line when logged in as the admin user, even without knowing that user credentials.

Currently the only way to do so from the CLI is to run `deis auth:cancel`, but that requires to log in as the user to be removed, and I may not have the password.

At the moment this seems to be possible only using the REST interface and regenerating a token, as said [here](http://docs.deis.io/en/latest/reference/api-v1.5/#cancel-account)

```

### What is wrong?

I'm planning to improve the developer guide for py-evm / trinity. I noticed that when I do a fresh clone, create a fresh `virtualenv` and run `pip install -e . -r requirements-dev.txt` I'm left with a system that doesn't have all dependencies installed to run the entire test suite without failures.

So, the TLDR of that issue is that it seems that our current developer guide doesn't leave you with a system that can satisfy all tests and hence isn't complete from a developers perspective.

In particular, at this point we are missing the `leveldb`, `web3`, `pysha3` and potentially other libs.

This happens both on my local system as well as in a fresh `python:3.6` docker container.

It doesn't seem to be a problem on CI though. My naive guess is that the tox setup triggers different installs.

The shortest path (that I'm aware of) to get all tests green is to run:

```
pip install -e .[trinity] -r requirements-dev.txt
pip install pysha3
```

At this point one should have all dependencies installed. 

I just want to double check if running the two commands above is something that is by design and the developer guide should highlight this or if our setup should be adjusted in any way. 

Or, maybe, if I'm just doing it wrong (in which case I really hope I wouldn't be the only person on the planet to run into this).

issue_title: ImportError: cannot import name 'version_info' of ruamel_yaml
issue_body:
## SOLUTION:

This is caused by an updating to a bad ruamel_yaml package, which has since been replaced in the repository.  To fix, you first need to rename all condarc configuration files so that conda does not need to parse any yaml.  The most common location for this file is a file named `.condarc` in your home directory.  After you rename that file, run

    conda update ruamel_yaml

After a successful update, you can rename your condarc file back to the original name and your install should be fixed.

---

At first, I installed Anaconda 3 5.0.1 64-bit on my Ubuntu 16.04. I recently updated the conda and its packages using:

`conda update conda`
`conda update --all`

Title is my theory more than anything.  With my new MKL packages, I have 5 builds so far:

```
conda search --override-channels -c msarahan mkl
Loading channels: done
Name                       Version                   Build  Channel
mkl                        2018.0.0             h1bd1d16_4  msarahan
mkl                        2018.0.0             h47df402_4  msarahan
mkl                        2018.0.0             h5077ac0_4  msarahan
mkl                        2018.0.0             h5f440b7_0  msarahan
mkl                        2018.0.0             hb1dadce_4  msarahan
```

h5077ac0_4 is the most recent.  If I run a test creation command, I get an older version:

```
conda create -n mkltest -c msarahan mkl

Solving environment: done

## Package Plan ##

  environment location: /Users/msarahan/miniconda3/envs/mkltest

    mkl:    2018.0.0-h1bd1d16_4 msarahan

Proceed ([y]/n)? n
```

The repodata entry for the h1bd1d16_4 build is here.  It has one dependency.

```
"mkl-2018.0.0-h1bd1d16_4.tar.bz2":{"timestamp":1502815658750,
"depends":["icc_rt"],"arch":"x86_64","operatingsystem":"darwin","target-triplet":"x86_64-any-darwin","size":155074161,"build_number":4,"name":"mkl","license":"proprietary - Intel","has_prefix":false,"license_family":"Proprietary","machine":"x86_64","platform":"osx","version":"2018.0.0","build":"h1bd1d16_4","binstar":{"package_id":"5993244f07006a12ce26bd22","channel":"main","owner_id":"55fac6e4d3234d09d295156c"},"md5":"3fa955642b0bc1873e3b407f2b5ee58b","requires":[],"subdir":"osx-64"}
```

The repodata for the current build, h5077ac0_4 is here.  It has two dependencies.

```
"mkl-2018.0.0-h5077ac0_4.tar.bz2":{"timestamp":1503615970929,
"depends":["icc_rt","intel-openmp"],
"arch":"x86_64","operatingsystem":"darwin","target-triplet":"x86_64-any-darwin","size":155039165,"build_number":4,"name":"mkl","license":"proprietary - Intel","has_prefix":false,"license_family":"Proprietary","machine":"x86_64","platform":"osx","version":"2018.0.0","build":"h5077ac0_4","binstar":{"package_id":"5993244f07006a12ce26bd22","channel":"main","owner_id":"55fac6e4d3234d09d295156c"},"md5":"6340dd0e05d8955c6b958df97e27733b","requires":[],"subdir":"osx-64"}

issue_title: Are packages supposed to have some kind of hash after the version number now?
issue_body:
I've searched documentation and issues and I can't seem to find anything about this change. I've been hesitant to go through with my conda update --all because it seems to want to just replace all my packages with the same version number but with a hash at the end, e.g. 

    jupyter:                  1.0.0-py36_3          --> 1.0.0-py36h9896ce5_0      
    jupyter_client:           5.1.0-py36_0          --> 5.1.0-py36h614e9ea_0      
    jupyter_core:             4.3.0-py36_0          --> 4.3.0-py36h357a921_0 

It's somewhat intimidating to all of a sudden have conda wanting to update and replace every single package without any sort of warning or apparent documentation of a big change in how package labeling works. And no big new version number of for conda itself? Even though they all claim to be coming from the 'defaults' channel, it just looks untrustworthy.

Is this an intended change and if it is, is there some documentation explaining why or what it means?

issue_title: Conda search output formatting
issue_body:
**I'm submitting a...** - [ ] bug report - [x] feature request ## Current Behavior `conda {search,list}` does some minimal formatting right now. While that works for short build strings, as they get longer, the formatting gets messed up. ### Steps to Reproduce ``` shraw@localhost:~$ conda search 'numpy 1.14.2 py36_nomklh2b20989_0' Loading channels: done # Name Version Build Channel numpy 1.14.2 py36_nomklh2b20989_0 pkgs/main ``` This might not be looking _that_ bad, but at our workplace we have custom packages with even longer build strings (compiler classifiers, cpp standard classifiers etc.). ## Expected Behavior I think the output can be formatted looking at the current shell's column size, similar to the `column` utility on linux. Maybe something similar too: 
# Name  Version                  Build  Channel
numpy    1.14.2   py36_nomklh2b20989_0  pkgs/main

issue_title: Check documentation examples
issue_body:
Some examples in the documentation are out of date https://github.com/Blosc/bcolz/blob/master/doc/tutorial.rst

In the following example one would not expect a void type in the ctable
```
>>> N = 100*1000
>>> ct = bcolz.fromiter(((i,i*i) for i in xrange(N)), dtype="i4,f8", count=N)
>>> ct
ctable((100000,), |V12) nbytes: 1.14 MB; cbytes: 283.27 KB; ratio: 4.14
  cparams := cparams(clevel=5, shuffle=True, cname='blosclz')
[(0, 0.0), (1, 1.0), (2, 4.0), ...,
 (99997, 9999400009.0), (99998, 9999600004.0), (99999, 9999800001.0)]
```
Instead one should get
```
ctable((100000,), [('f0', '<i4'), ('f1', '<f8')])
  nbytes: 1.14 MB; cbytes: 675.24 KB; ratio: 1.74
  cparams := cparams(clevel=5, shuffle=True, cname='blosclz')
[(0, 0.0) (1, 1.0) (2, 4.0) ..., (99997, 9999400009.0)
 (99998, 9999600004.0) (99999, 9999800001.0)]
```

issue_title: Keep header when file is closed
issue_body:
After closing an `AlignmenFile`, the `reference_name` attribute of `AlignedSegment` instances from that file cannot be accessed anymore, because the header data has been deleted. Example:

```python
from pysam import AlignmentFile

def load(path):
    with AlignmentFile(path) as bam:
        return next(bam)

al = load('some.bam')
al.reference_name
```

The last line results in the following error:

```
ValueError: I/O operation on closed file
```

Would it be possible to keep the header information around to support that operation after the file is closed? Would it be possible to store the reference names as Python strings in a tuple, and reference that in `AlignedSegment`? This way Python's own resource management mechanism would handle it.

issue_title: Performance: importing the redis command module is slow
issue_body: The redis command module preemptively loads the redis management library. This will slow down scenarios like running 'az' with no arguments, as well as any other scenario where we load all command packages to find the right command implementation.

issue_title: Provision of Azure Function APP with Consumption Plan
issue_body:
I have been trying to create Azure Function App with Consumption with the code below, however, after debugging for days, I still couldn't get my code working. So eventually, I had to fall back to ARM templates again. Can you tell me if provision Function App via Azure Python SDK is supported or not?

import os
import sys
import argparse
import subprocess
import requests
from azure.mgmt.web import WebSiteManagementClient
from azure.mgmt.resource import ResourceManagementClient
from azure.common.credentials import ServicePrincipalCredentials
import xml.etree.ElementTree as ET

parser = argparse.ArgumentParser(
    description='parse option arguments passed to this script',
    epilog='you can write to jianhua.wu@agridigital.io for help',
    prefix_chars='-'
    )

parser.add_argument('--SubscriptionId', required = True, help = 'your Azure Subscrition ID for resource creation')
parser.add_argument('--ClientId', required = True, help='your Azure App Registration ID')
parser.add_argument('--SecretId', required = True, help='the secret id of your Azure App Registration ID')

parsed_argu = parser.parse_args(sys.argv[1:])

subscription_id = parsed_argu.SubscriptionId

    deploy_function_app()

issue_title: Consider separating container public access from get/set_container_acl
issue_body:
Currently if you want to know the public access level of a container, you have to use the `get_container_acl` call. One would reasonably expect that `get_container_acl` would return the ACL only, not the ACL and maybe some other properties too. Public access seems like it would logically be returned by the `get_container_properties` call since it is a property of the container itself, whereas the ACL is a list of stored settings for use in generating SAS tokens.

Another scenario in which this conflation of the two concepts is painful is when you just want to update the public access policy for a container. This can easily be done in the portal, but if you try:
```python
set_container_acl(container, access_policy='blob')
```
This will result in wiping out your container's ACL, which almost certainly wasn't your intent. Instead, you have to do a call to `get_container_acl` (even though you don't want to change anything) and then pass the signed identifiers back in to the call to `set_container_acl` to perserve them.

There isn't a call for `set_container_properties` but it might be good from a usability standpoint to consider creating one and splitting out that functionality.

issue_title: StreamResponse instances are all equal
issue_body:
## Long story short

Since #2494 , `StreamResponse` inherits `collections.MutableMapping.__eq__`, which makes them basically all equal. 
The implementation in `Mapping` looks like `return dict(self.items()) == dict(other.items())`.

This is especially the case for `WebSocketResponse` : when following https://aiohttp.readthedocs.io/en/stable/faq.html#how-do-i-programmatically-close-a-websocket-server-side, if you use a `list` instead of a `set`, it's impossible to `remove` the correct websocket.

## Expected behaviour

```python
>>> from aiohttp.web_ws import WebSocketResponse
>>> r1 = WebSocketResponse()
>>> r2 = WebSocketResponse()
>>> r1 == r2
False
>>> id(r1) == id(r2)
False
>>> r1 is r2
False
>>> hash(r1) == hash(r2)
False
```

As a rule, `a == b` implies `hash(a) == hash(b)`. But it's now broken.

## Actual behaviour

Since v3.0:
```python
>>> r1 == r2
True
>>> id(r1) == id(r2)
False
>>> r1 is r2
False
>>> hash(r1) == hash(r2)
False
```

## Steps to reproduce

Described above

## Your environment

* `aiohttp >= 3.0`

issue_title: web.run_app(): Terminate properly with signal TERM and INT
issue_body:
## Long story short

This is more or less related to #63. My issue is that `web.run_app()` gracefully exit when SIGINT is sent to the process but not when SIGTERM is sent.

This is because `web.run_app()` catches SIGINT (with `KeyboardInterrupt` exception) but does not override SIGTERM handling (there is no exception raised for that, one can only use `signal.signal`). Most of the time it is SIGTERM that is used to gracefully shutdown a process but that will actually kill the application instead of letting the requests finish properly and response. `kill`, Docker and system.d use SIGTERM by default.

## Expected behaviour

I suppose the default expected behavior of `web.run_app()` is to allow the requests to be finished before terminating the process. That's what most people would think it does. The feeling is also harder since it does it already for SIGINT.

## Actual behaviour

Only SIGINT triggers a graceful exit. SIGTERM does not let the running requests finish.

## Steps to reproduce

1. run this script
```
import asyncio
from aiohttp import web

async def handle(request):
    await asyncio.sleep(60)
    text = "Hello"
    return web.Response(text=text)

app = web.Application()
app.router.add_get('/', handle)

web.run_app(app)
```
2. do a GET request on /
3. send SIGTERM to the process
4. you will notice the process ends before the 60 seconds and your request has been canceled

## Your environment

Python 3.6, Arch Linux

issue_title: Proposal: remove solution cells
issue_body:
I think the concept of solution cells is confusing, because you can just make the solution region (e.g. with `### BEGIN SOLUTION`), and so then it's less clear what the difference between solution cells and grade cells is. I propose getting rid of the solution cell checkbox, and just requiring people to use the solution region annotation. Then, in the cell toolbar, we can change the "grade?" checkbox to be different for markdown vs code cells:

* for markdown cells, the label should be something like "written answer?"
* for code cells, the label should be something like "tests for code answer?"

Under the hood, we'll still have the concept of solution cells and grade cells, but I think in the UI the different pieces are just confusing.

For my proposed new cell type (#131), we could add a checkbox that has a label saying "prevent edits?" or something like that. It would automatically get checked on code cells that are grade cells, and would be impossible to check on markdown cells that are grade cells.

@ellisonbg what are your thoughts on this?

issue_title: Use tuple not list for __all__ attributes
issue_body:
TravisCI doctring linting reports loads of warnings like this:

```
planemo/conda.py WARNING: __all__ is defined as a list, this means pydocstyle cannot reliably detect contents of the __all__ variable, because it can be mutated. Change __all__ to be an (immutable) tuple, to remove this warning. Note, pydocstyle uses __all__ to detect which definitions are public, to warn if public definitions are missing docstrings. If __all__ is a (mutable) list, pydocstyle cannot reliably assume its contents. pydocstyle will proceed assuming __all__ is not mutated.
```

Switching ``__all__`` from a list to a tuple ought to fix this?

issue_title: Improvements to docker install instructions
issue_body: I want to run a full node on a ubuntu virtual machine. It's 16.0.4.3 LTS. I'm trying to follow the docker instructions on https://github.com/blockstack/blockstack-core#install-with-docker My background is: I know what docker is. I recall installing it before as part of the discourse forum software install instructions. I've heard it makes setting up server side apps easier because you don't have to manage a bunch of dependencies. I don't use it regularly though. I'm intentionally following instructions as they are written even when I know they won't work. --- I downloaded the `launcher` script by clicking on "download" but it didn't download, it opened in my browser. I realized I probably need to copy and past the script text into a file on the server. I try to run `./launcher pull`. I get an error. Ahh I need to make the script executable. Instructions didn't tell me that. Another error. Hmm I don't have docker installed. The instructions didn't tell me to install docker or how I can do that. I try `apt-get install docker`. It installs something. yay! I have docker! Try to run `./launcher pull`. Hmm..the same error `docker` not found. I guess that wasn't docker? By now I'm lost. The next step is i have to go down a rabbit hole of figuring out what docker is (because apparently `apt-get install docker` wasn't it and how to install it. If i'm really motivated and have a lot of time, I'll go figure out how to install docker by googling around. But I came here trying to learn about blockstack, not docker. Our docker install instructions were supposed to make it faster and easier than the `pip install` instructions, but it didn't succeed in doing that. --- I think docker install for running full code nodes on servers has the potential to make this a lot easier for everyone. It would be great if we can review the instructions and make them super easy to follow for someone with basic linux command line ability. Discourse does this very well with their instructions for installing discourse in the cloud via docker. All you have to do is exactly follow the steps they give you and you get a discourse. My suggestion would be to approach our instructions in the same way. Some one should be able to follow them step by step without having to assume anything more than a general ability to use the unix command line. I really like how the discourse instructions tell you exactly what version of what operating system you should install. I don't think we need to go so far as push people to a particular cloud vendor, but telling them to start with a Ubuntu 16.04 LTS install might be a good idea so that everyone is on the same page.

issue_title: Empty $TRAVIS_PYTHON_NAME included regardless of --env / -e
issue_body:
It seems (possibly due to a change on TravisCI), that ``$TRAVIS_PYTHON_NAME`` is currently empty, e.g.

https://codecov.io/gh/ipython/ipython/commit/c17ff28cb1220a91d94fc0dc4b85ccee561fb68f/builds

https://codecov.io/gh/biopython/biopython/commit/2d7619594003460b61fab62260ce50f6a344ba63/builds

Reading the code for ``--env`` aka ``-e``, the current behaviour is to automatically add some additional environment variables over-riding those specified at the command line, e.g. for TravisCI

``` python
include_env.add('TRAVIS_OS_NAME')
include_env.add('TRAVIS_%s_NAME' % language.upper())
include_env.add('TOXENV')
```

and for Snap:

``` python
include_env.add(os.getenv('DISPLAY'))
```

Having these as the default is nice (if ``-env`` or ``-e`` is not used), but to me having them over-ride the command line is surprising. As per #75 the documentation here could be clearer?

issue_title: Fix nsqlookupd 1.0 compatible queries

issue_title: make prepare creates s3am but it is not in the .gitignore
Link fate of nodes in subclusters
issue_body:
Depends on #703

Related to #644

1) Assume there is a Toil cluster with one or more Spark/HDFS subclusters. If one of the Spark/HDFS master processes fails, the service job representing it should also fail (#703).

2) When that service job fails, the service jobs that represent the Spark/HDFS slaves connected to that Spark/HDFS master should fail. As it is now I think the slave processes and their representative services will just keep running (this issue).

3) When the master service job fails, the driver job using it will fail.

4) On a subcluster with replication factor R, if the right combination of R Spark/HDFS workers die, a block of HDFS data is lost. This leads to driver failure but it may take a while before that situation is discovered.

5) If the driver job fails, the entire cluster should be taken down (#644).

issue_title: dotnet core RHEL-x64 publish output doesn't work with RHEL 6.x versions
issue_body:
When we build and publish self contained application with runtime identifier rhel-x64 and tried to use it on RHEL 6.x platform, we get following error

"dlopen failed to open the libcoreclr.so with error /lib64/libc.so.6: version `GLIBC_2.14' not found"

If we publish with rhel.6-x64 identifier and use these published binaries, then it works correctly on RHEL 6.x platform.

We are using dotnet core SDK 2.1.4 with runtime version 2.0.4 which supports RHEL 6.x platform as per below link
https://github.com/dotnet/core/blob/master/Documentation/build-and-install-rhel6-prerequisites.md

We expect binaries published with portable runtime identifier to work for all versions of that specific platform.

issue_title: Troubles to set up internal NuGet server for .NET Core development/publishing
issue_body:
A company can block internet access for its internal development machines. In such cases, an internal NuGet server would be required to host the necessary packages, so as to enable .NET Core development and publishing.

I built a detailed example and documented it in [my blog post](https://blog.lextudio.com/offline-publishing-asp-net-core-apps-cf22f45158af), which shows how troublesome the experience is today, when attempting to publish the default ASP.NET Core MVC project. 

To summarize,

* The default NuGet source setting specifies nuget.org, which if blocked leads to an error. This error can only be resolved by adding a custom nuget.config file, which is not pleasant.
* `dotnet publish -r win-x64` would generate errors on missing packages, which is good, but there is no easy way to extract a list of NuGet packages (with version numbers) out and download them. Ideally, such can be done by a PowerShell script, but no sample anyway.
* The `Detected package downgrade` error message can be misleading, while the fix is to explicitly set `<RuntimeFrameworkVersion>`.

Hope there can be official guides from Microsoft to show how such internal NuGet server should be set up (especially which packages are mandatory). That can save tons of hours for those companies to explore on their own.

issue_title: Sockets not listening right Internet Protocol in Net classes
issue_body:
# Sockets not listening right Internet Protocol in Net classes

Currently the (tested) sockets wrappers won't use IPv6 alongside IPv4 in dual-stack.
Meaning out of the box connections on SmtpClient, TcpClient and MySql connector require modifications at the Socket for functioning on IPv6. 

In .NET Standard this isn't the issue at all because it determines the right protocol by the given input string already.
Source:
https://blogs.msdn.microsoft.com/webdev/2013/01/08/dual-mode-sockets-never-create-an-ipv4-socket-again/

This SHOULD be in .NET core aswell as this might prevent starting or even experienced developers from using IPv6 over all as IPv4 'Just works'.

# Environment

Version: .NET-Core v2.1.105
Project: WebAPI
Platform: Windows 10 Pro x86-x64
      Build: 16299.371

# Testing
## SmtpClient
Code:
```c#
SmtpClient smtpClient= new SmtpClient();
smtpClient.DeliveryMethod = SmtpDeliveryMethod.Network;

smtpClient.Port = 587;
smtpClient.Host = "fd00::8:23";
smtpClient.EnableSsl = true;

smtpClient.UseDefaultCredentials = false;
smtpClient.Credentials = new NetworkCredential("username", "password");

MailMessage mmMail = new MailMessage("valid_sender", "valid_receiver");
mmMail.Subject = "Subject";
mmMail.Body = "Message";

smtpClient.SendMailAsync(Mail);
```
Resulting in:
```
System.Net.Mail.SmtpException: Failure sending mail. ---> System.ArgumentException: None of the discovered or specified addresses match the socket address family.
Parameter name: context
   at System.Runtime.ExceptionServices.ExceptionDispatchInfo.Throw()
   at System.Net.Sockets.Socket.DoMultipleAddressConnectCallback(Object result, MultipleAddressConnectAsyncResult context)
   at System.Net.Sockets.Socket.DoDnsCallback(IAsyncResult result, MultipleAddressConnectAsyncResult context)
   at System.Net.Sockets.Socket.DnsCallback(IAsyncResult result)
--- End of stack trace from previous location where exception was thrown ---
   at System.Runtime.ExceptionServices.ExceptionDispatchInfo.Throw()
   at System.Net.Sockets.Socket.EndConnect(IAsyncResult asyncResult)
   at System.Net.Sockets.TcpClient.EndConnect(IAsyncResult asyncResult)
   at System.Net.Mail.SmtpConnection.ConnectAndHandshakeAsyncResult.InitializeConnectionCallback(IAsyncResult result)
--- End of stack trace from previous location where exception was thrown ---
   at System.Runtime.ExceptionServices.ExceptionDispatchInfo.Throw()
   at System.Net.Mail.SmtpConnection.ConnectAndHandshakeAsyncResult.End(IAsyncResult result)
   at System.Net.Mail.SmtpTransport.EndGetConnection(IAsyncResult result)
   at System.Net.Mail.SmtpClient.ConnectCallback(IAsyncResult result)
```
Doing this synchronically using:
```c#
smtpClient.Send(Mail);
```
Results:
```
System.Net.Sockets.SocketException (0x80004005): A request to send or receive data was disallowed because the socket is not connected and (when sending on a datagram socket using a sendto call) no address was supplied
   at System.Net.Sockets.TcpClient.Connect(String hostname, Int32 port)
   at System.Net.Mail.SmtpConnection.GetConnection(String host, Int32 port)
   at System.Net.Mail.SmtpTransport.GetConnection(String host, Int32 port)
   at System.Net.Mail.SmtpClient.GetConnection()
   at System.Net.Mail.SmtpClient.Send(MailMessage message)
```

## MySQL
Using a simple custom (working wrapper) with the following querystring
```c#
this.connectionString = "Server=fd00::8:21;Port=3306;Uid=Username;Pwd=password;Allow User Variables=True;Connection Timeout=1;";
```
I get the following error:
```
System.NotSupportedException: This protocol version is not supported.
   at System.Net.Sockets.Socket.BeginConnect(IPAddress address, Int32 port, AsyncCallback requestCallback, Object state)
   at System.Net.Sockets.Socket.BeginConnect(String host, Int32 port, AsyncCallback requestCallback, Object state)
   at System.Net.Sockets.TcpClient.BeginConnect(String host, Int32 port, AsyncCallback requestCallback, Object state)
   at System.Net.Sockets.TcpClient.<>c.<ConnectAsync>b__28_0(String targetHost, Int32 targetPort, AsyncCallback callback, Object state)
   at System.Threading.Tasks.TaskFactory`1.FromAsyncImpl[TArg1,TArg2](Func`5 beginMethod, Func`2 endFunction, Action`1 endAction, TArg1 arg1, TArg2 arg2, Object state, TaskCreationOptions creationOptions)
   at System.Threading.Tasks.TaskFactory.FromAsync[TArg1,TArg2](Func`5 beginMethod, Action`1 endMethod, TArg1 arg1, TArg2 arg2, Object state)
   at System.Net.Sockets.TcpClient.ConnectAsync(String host, Int32 port)
   at MySql.Data.Common.StreamCreator.GetTcpStream(MySqlConnectionStringBuilder settings)
   at MySql.Data.MySqlClient.NativeDriver.Open()
```

## Plain TcpClient
Leading me to do some testing using a TcpClient:
```c#
TcpClient tcpClient = new TcpClient();
IPAddress ipaAddress = IPAddress.Parse("fd00::8:23");
tcpClient.Connect(ipaAddress, 587);
```
Result:
```
System.Net.Internals.SocketExceptionFactory.ExtendedSocketException
  HResult=0x80004005
  Message=An address incompatible with the requested protocol was used [fd00::8:23]:587
  Source=System.Net.Sockets
  StackTrace:
   at System.Net.Sockets.Socket.DoConnect(EndPoint endPointSnapshot, SocketAddress socketAddress)
   at System.Net.Sockets.Socket.Connect(EndPoint remoteEP)
   at System.Net.Sockets.TcpClient.Connect(IPEndPoint remoteEP)
   at System.Net.Sockets.TcpClient.Connect(IPAddress address, Int32 port)
```

If I set the AddressFamily however the code works:
```c#
TcpClient tcpClient = new TcpClient(AddressFamily.InterNetworkV6);
```

issue_title: ACME client for Windows/IIS
issue_body:
I propose developing an ACME client for Windows + IIS.

Here are some of my thoughts:

* Create a PowerShell-based solution, this would be comparable to the Python-based one already in the works for Linux/Apache ([letsencrypt.py](https://github.com/letsencrypt/lets-encrypt-preview)): 
  * This will also be allow more compatibility with more environments, such as Windows Core, and even remote execution 
* Leverage the existing Windows components for any dependencies, for example under the hood, letsencrypt.py uses OpenSSL for all the cert management stuff, whereas on Windows you could use the certreq CLI or the CertEnroll API. 
* By structuring the code/libs nicely, we can make parts reusable outside of the main script, for example API/library for the ACME protocol itself to be used in other implementations or projects.
* Try to mimic the letsencrypt.py command-line usage as much as possible/practical so that there is consistency across platforms [letsencrypt.py CLI usage](https://github.com/letsencrypt/lets-encrypt-preview#command-line-usage)

issue_title: AvroProducer: Attempt to pull schema from schema-registry if not cached during publish
issue_body:
It is currently only possible to publish to a topic if the schema is specified.  It would be nice to be able to have an `AvroProducer` attempt to pull the latest schema from the schema-registry if there is a schema cache miss.
The schema could be referenced by subject i.e. 'topic-value' or by schema id.
The workaround is to do a GET request to the schema-registry and then pass the returned schema into the producer call, but it seems cleaner to have the registry cache handle it.

If this is of interest, I have modified the behavior of `AvroProducer.produce` to also accept a schema id parameter such that if it is specified the registry cache will fetch the schema if available.
It is a simple change since the behavior was already written into the registry cache class.
I did not modify `AvroProducer.produce` to try to fetch a missing schema by subject, since this seemed to conflict with a test case.

Given this CWL:

```class: CommandLineTool
cwlVersion: v1.0

inputs:
  pattern:
    type: string
    inputBinding: {position: 0}
  infile:
    type: File

baseCommand: grep
```

and this input:

```infile: testfile.txt
pattern: Requirement
```

run with this cwltool:

```$ cwltool --version
/usr/local/bin/cwltool 1.0.20161216212910```

I get this:

```Got workflow error
Traceback (most recent call last):
  File "/Library/Python/2.7/site-packages/cwltool/main.py", line 225, in single_job_executor
    for r in jobiter:
  File "/Library/Python/2.7/site-packages/cwltool/draft2tool.py", line 243, in job
    builder = self._init_job(joborder, **kwargs)

issue_title: Saving FKs breaks Authorization
issue_body:
At https://github.com/toastdriven/django-tastypie/blob/master/tastypie/resources.py#L1857, tastypie calls `save_related` "just in case".  This causes problems with reasonable Authorization behavior, as it is impossible for a validly created resource to have a read-only resource as a foreign key.

For more specificity, the bug appears in the following situation:


I have:

 - A __Note__ resource representing a note left by a user.  Only the user who created a Note can edit it. 
 - A __Comment__ resource.  Comments can be left on any note by any user.

__TL;DR:__ I am unable to limit Note editing to a Note's creator while still allowing any user to comment on a Note.

I am using the following setup for Authentication:

    class CreatedByEditAuthorization(Authorization):
        def is_authorized(self, request, object=None, **kwargs):
            return True
    
        def apply_limits(self, request, object_list):
            if request and request.method != 'GET' and hasattr(request, 'user'):
                return object_list.filter(created_by=request.user)
            return object_list

In short, a user is only authorized to edit objects for which they are equal to the created_by property (they can only edit the objects they created).

This is linked as follows:

    class NoteResource(ModelResource):
        comments = fields.ToManyField('myapp.api.resources.CommentResource', 'comments', null=True, blank=True)
        created_by = fields.ToOneField('account.api.resources.UserResource', 'created_by')
    
        # Attach the user on creation
        def obj_create(self, bundle, request, **kwargs):
            return super(NoteResource, self).obj_create(bundle, request, created_by=request.user)
    
        class Meta:
            queryset = Note.objects.all()
            allowed_methods = ['get', 'put', 'post']
            authorization = CreatedByEditAuthorization()

so here, when an object is created, I automatically attach the current user to the `created_by` attribute and link it to the proper Authorization.

A `Comment` resource is simple and just has a `ForeignKey` to a `Note` resource.

__The problem is this:__  if user A creates a Note and user B attempts to create a comment with that Note as a Foreign Key, tastypie does an update (by calling `save_related`) on that Note.  That attempt is rejected as user B did not create the Note, so it doesn't pass Authorization.

__There are two potential solutions:__

- Provide a way to tell a `Resource` not to `save_related` on a given ForeignKey
- Pass along additional information to the Authorization so it knows if `is_authorized` was requested as a result of `save_related`.

issue_body:
I have not been able to set private=True and public=False.

I've setup a custom cache like this and what I'm aiming for is

issue_title: Tastypie returns a list of cached objects to unauthenticated users
issue_body:
This issue is in the context of a locked down Resource with Authentication/Authorization set to require a logged in user for GET requests.

If a project is using the Django caching middleware, tastypie will return cached results (and status_code 200) to unauthenticated users instead of a 401 error.

The work around is setting `CACHE_MIDDLEWARE_ANONYMOUS_ONLY = True`

I'm not sure if this is possible to fix, since the caching middleware probably returns the response before the request ever hits tastypie.  It should however be documented.

Jupyter notebook has a toolbar on top, but those are mostly per cell operations, applied on the cell active at the time.

issue_title: Downloading more than one video breaks next page command
issue_body:
<!--- Provide a general summary of the issue in the Title above -->

## Issue / Suggestion

Steps to reproduce:
- Make a query or a user search
- Download at least 2 videos from the results page: `dv x,y`
- Ask for the next results page: `n`

Expected result: see the next results page

issue_title: job.copy has some slight problems
issue_body:
performing a job.copy on a job which is in a ```completed``` state will potentially give a bad ```outputfiles``` section in the new job.

For a completed job with ```*.txt``` for instance as a namePattern the completed job contains the resolved file from this job and a copy will return a new file which is fully resolved in the ```outputfiles``` which makes sense in terms of copying the actual value of the ```outputfiles``` list.

Should we consider making the ```outputfiles``` empty for jobs which are copying from ```completed``` jobs and inform the user that this is being erased?

This isn't something too serious but it would cause a ```job.copy().submit()``` to fail which is a tad irritating.

issue_title: Should we make querystrings reserved words?
incorporate bandwidths into CWT wavelet names for families `cmor`, `shan`, `fbsp`
issue_body:
The `cmor`, `shan` and `fbsp` `ContinuousWavelet` objects have both a `bandwidth_frequency` and `center_frequency` attribute.  If a user calls the `cwt` routine in Matlab these must be specified in the name as follows:

e.g. `cmor1.5-1` gives a complex Morlet wavelet with `bandwidth_frequency` of 1.5 and `center_frequency` of 1.0.

To do the equivalent in PyWavelets one currently has to first create a wavelet and then manually modify the attributes:
```Python
w = pywt.ContinuousWavelet('cmor')
w.bandwidth_frequency=1.5
w.center_frequency=1
```

If the user passes just `'cmor'` to the `cwt` routine in Matlab an error would be raised as the bandwidths are not specified.  In PyWavelets, this currently does not raise an error and default values are set  (these appear to be equivalent to `'cmor1.0-0.5' in Matlab).

I propose to allow parsing the 2 bandwidth parameters from the Wavelet names as is done in Matlab.

I think it is also probably good to **require** that these be specified than choosing some undocumented default.  (i.e. raise an exception if just the blank wavelet family name such as `cmor` is passed into `cwt` or `ContinuousWavelet`)

The default setting has already caused some confusion as in #307

issue_title: Dual-tree complex wavelet transform
issue_body:
Hi all,

Some discussions on implementing the dual-tree complex wavelet transform in PyWavelets occurred in #58 , but let me restate some things:

The dual-tree complex wavelet transform is described in a [review paper](http://ieeexplore.ieee.org/document/1550194/). A Matlab implementation is available [here](http://eeweb.poly.edu/iselesni/), and a [Python port](https://github.com/rjw57/dtcwt) is available.

I needed to implement the dual-tree complex wavelet transform (DTCWT) for my research. I have made the code public [here](https://github.com/LaurentRDC/dualtree) (Python 3 only). I would like to discuss the inclusion of the DTCWT in PyWavelets if there is enough interest.

issue_body:
| Required Info                         |                                                                |

| Operating System & Version |   Win 10  | 
| Platform                                 | PC  |
| SDK Version                            |  v2.10.4                          | 

Hello! I'm trying to detect dropped frames. I'm currently looking at the timestamp delta between two frames to see if it's longer than the framerate (plus a little wiggle room), which is working fine. For example, for 30fps the delta should be 33ms, so I set it to 40ms.

I'm also noticing that `pipeline.wait_for_frames(40)` (using the same timeout duration as above) times out as well as expected. My issue is how can I differentiate this instance from other errors that cause `wait_for_frames()` to fail such as a device disconnection?

Looking at the source for librealsense, here is the line where that error is thrown:
https://github.com/IntelRealSense/librealsense/blob/v2.10.4/src/pipeline.cpp#L598

I would like to not rely on string comparisons for "Frame didn't arrived." I see that there is an timeout value in the notification enum here, but I'm not sure how to receive this notification, or if it's even thrown in this case:
https://github.com/IntelRealSense/librealsense/blob/v2.10.4/include/librealsense2/h/rs_types.h#L18

Is it possible that `wait_for_frames()` could instead do something like return an empty frameset rather than throw an error, or attach some enum value to the error that does get thrown?

Thank you!

issue_title: Understanding normalization of advantage function
issue_body:
I am wondering about the normalization of the advantage function in PPO.  Before training on a batch the mean of the advantage function is subtracted and it's divided by its std. 

To me it makes intuitively sense to divide the advantage function by its std since then we always have the same magnitude in gradients in each update. However, I totally don't understand why it would be beneficial to substract the mean from the advantage function. In my understanding that would introduce some form of bias since now for example values that were greater 0 and that should be encouraged to do more often can now potential fall below 0 if they are smaller than the mean -> these values are falsely trained to do less often.

So whats the intuition behind substracting the mean? And does it really improve learning?

issue_title: analysis: Thread1.1 6.3.2 Network data update
issue_body: 6.3.2 test case fails when checking if CIDs corresponding to all configured on-mesh prefix are present in Address Registration TLV in Child Update Request message. Currently for sleepy end device, after it obtains the new network data(i.e. on-mesh prefix propagated from border router), it will send two child update request messages continuously to its parent. The first child update message occurs in `HandleNetworkDataUpdate` after sleepy end device set the new network data; and the second one occurs in `HandleNetifStateChanged` after a new IPv6 unicast address added on thread interface. Harness will locate the first child update message after leader multicast a Data Response with new configured on-mesh prefix(i.e. 2001::/64). However, for the first child update message, Address Registration TLV still does not include the new configured IPv6 unicast address, hence, it does not include CID neither. The screenshot as below: Notice that Thread 1.1 spec 4.7.3 states: "A child update Request may be sent by a child in the following circumstances: To modify its mode, timeout duration, and/or address registration" @jwhui @librasungirl Is it essential to send the first child update request in `HandleNetworkDataUpdate` for a sleepy end device? or if there is another scenario that require to send the child update request in `HandleNetworkDataUpdate`, please let me know. Thanks.

issue_title: Never emits creds to stdout any more
issue_body:
The profile support changes broke emitting credentials to stdout -- if no profile is specified, it always uses the name `default` which results in updating a credentials file.

This also breaks the dockerised workflow, because the credentials file that's updated is inside the container, instead of being the user's credentials file.

issue_title: Add diff support for other files types and directories
issue_body:
```python

def do_diff(args):
    ''' Compare two configuration files. '''
    # Todo:  Allow fallback to text comparisons for non-conf files.
    # Todo:  Eventually support directory (or recursive?) diffs too.
    parse_args = dict(dup_stanza=args.duplicate_stanza, dup_key=args.duplicate_key,
                      keep_comments=args.comments, strict=True)
    # Parse all config files
    try:
        cfg1 = parse_conf(args.conf1, **parse_args)
        cfg2 = parse_conf(args.conf2, **parse_args)
    except IOError, e:
        sys.stderr.write("Unable to open conf file.  {}\n".format(e.message))
        return EXIT_CODE_NO_SUCH_FILE
    except ConfParserException, e:
        sys.stderr.write("Unable to parse conf file.  {}\n".format(e))
        # We don't distinguish between bad conf files and safety checks here.
        return EXIT_CODE_BAD_CONF_FILE
    diffs = compare_cfgs(cfg1, cfg2)
    rc = show_diff(args.output, diffs, headers=(args.conf1, args.conf2))
    if rc == EXIT_CODE_DIFF_EQUAL:
        sys.stderr.write("Files are the same.\n")
    elif rc == EXIT_CODE_DIFF_NO_COMMON:
        sys.stderr.write("No common stanzas between files.\n")
    return rc
```

issue_title: Selected the wrong server block in Nginx
issue_body:
Part of the reason for #4087 was multiple server blocks for the same `server_name`, IP, and port. We'd enable a redirect to the wrong server block and fail the test.

While tricky, I believe we could correctly handle this case someday. Based upon [this](https://www.digitalocean.com/community/tutorials/understanding-nginx-server-and-location-block-selection-algorithms), I believe in cases like this Nginx chooses the first matching server block (if someone works on this they should verify this is the case in all versions of Nginx we support). Additionally, since [Nginx 1.3.10](https://nginx.org/en/CHANGES), the order files are added via an `included` directive is well defined. We currently [support as old as Nginx 1.3.7](https://github.com/certbot/certbot/blob/ed717d6bc483dff49f3d6fe1f9d7ee973127ac29/certbot-nginx/certbot_nginx/configurator.py#L558), but we could probably bump this requirement to 1.3.10 if no major OSes are still using this version.

issue_title: Nginx should make a vhost block in the absence of a appropriate one
issue_body: As mentioned in #4792 nginx gives a somewhat useless error when you have a default configuration lacking a server_name directive with the appropriate domain name. While documenting how to mitigate this is useful, we should probably just handle this case - by making an appropriate vhost, like we do with apache and running the challenge.

issue_title: Splitting PyDatalab into Runtime and Notebook packages.
issue_body:
In some cases where users want to use Python BQ APIs (or maybe ML APIs) as a pure library (such as running in a service), the notebook related features are not needed, hence it does not make sense to have IPython, Matplotlib, etc as dependencies.

After split, the runtime part should depend on only limited non-UI libraries such as numpy, pandas. The notebook part will depend on runtime, as well as other UI related libraries such as matplotlib, IPython, etc.

issue_title: HttpAccessTokenRefreshError: invalid_grant: Token has been revoked
issue_body:
I noticed an issue where `context.credentials.get_access_token()` does not return the most recent oauth2client access token.

Steps to reproduce
1) Run datalab locally and sign in. Confirm that a BigQuery SQL query works
2) Remove the access which was granted to `Google Cloud SDK` in your [Google Account Security Settings](https://security.google.com/settings/security/secureaccount)
3) Confirm that a BigQuery SQL query now fails with error `invalid_grant: Token has been revoked.`
4) Token is now revoked, so I sign in again in the same local datalab environment.
5) Since I just signed in, I expect BigQuery sql to work with the new access token. I still get the error  `invalid_grant: Token has been revoked.`

```
>from datalab.context import Context
>context = Context.default().credentials.get_access_token()
HttpAccessTokenRefreshErrorTraceback (most recent call last)
<ipython-input-16-db0a2becc447> in <module>()
      1 from datalab.context import Context
      2 
----> 3 context = Context.default().credentials.get_access_token()

/usr/local/lib/python2.7/dist-packages/oauth2client/client.pyc in get_access_token(self, http)
    773             if not http:
    774                 http = httplib2.Http()
--> 775             self.refresh(http)
    776         return AccessTokenInfo(access_token=self.access_token,
    777                                expires_in=self._expires_in())

/usr/local/lib/python2.7/dist-packages/oauth2client/client.pyc in refresh(self, http)
    656                   request.
    657         """
--> 658         self._refresh(http.request)
    659 
    660     def revoke(self, http):

/usr/local/lib/python2.7/dist-packages/oauth2client/client.pyc in _refresh(self, http_request)
    861         """
    862         if not self.store:
--> 863             self._do_refresh_request(http_request)
    864         else:
    865             self.store.acquire_lock()

/usr/local/lib/python2.7/dist-packages/oauth2client/client.pyc in _do_refresh_request(self, http_request)
    930             except (TypeError, ValueError):
    931                 pass
--> 932             raise HttpAccessTokenRefreshError(error_msg, status=resp.status)
    933 
    934     def _revoke(self, http_request):

HttpAccessTokenRefreshError: invalid_grant: Token has been revoked.
``

I've confirmed that oauth2client returns the correct token, which is still valid. Here is the test code:

```
import oauth2client
auth_header = "Authorization:\ Bearer\ " + oauth2client.client.GoogleCredentials.get_application_default().get_access_token()[0]
!curl -H "Metadata-Flavor: Google" -H $auth_header https://www.googleapis.com/oauth2/v1/userinfo
```

It seems it is unlikely that users will hit this specific issue. An additional possibility is that the access token expires. The user signs in successfully, but datalab is still using the expired access token.

```

I tracked the problem down to be a problem in the deletion `Collector` class where related object to `Farm` (i.e. `Animal`) are of the sub-classes either `Dog` or `Cat`. So in the case above, we try to delete the `Cat` object from the `Dog` table.

I solved my problem by using the `_base_manager` property of the `Model` class . And referring to a non polymorphic object manager like below 
```python
class Animal(PolymorphicModel):
    instance = models.ForeignKey('Farm', on_delete=models.CASCADE)
    name = models.CharField(max_length=100)
    _base_manager = models.Manager()
    class Meta:
        pass
```

Now when trying to delete the `Farm` object in admin I get the correct hierarchy

- Farm: Farm number 19
  - Animal: #37 - dog
    - Dog: #37 - dog
  - Animal: #38 - cat
    - Cat: #38 - cat

I am using the below 
```bash
psql 9.3.11
python 2.7.6
django 1.9.8
django-polymorphic 0.9.2
```

My thought would be to add `_base_manager = models.Manager()` in the django-polymorphic `PolymorphicModel`. What do you guy think about this ?

Thanks,
Theo

issue_title: Trouble with parallelizing PyTorch code
issue_body:
### System information
- **OS Platform and Distribution (e.g., Linux Ubuntu 16.04)**: macos 10.11.6
- **Ray installed from (source or binary)**:  `pip`
- **Ray version**: 0.4.0
- **Python version**: 3.6.4
- **Exact command to reproduce**: See [this gist](https://gist.github.com/7c4bd9859f91cffcbca2746e5bad89a8) and try to run it with `python3 single.py`.

### Describe the problem
<!-- Describe the problem clearly here. -->
I'm trying to adapt my serial version of [OpenAI's REPTILE](https://blog.openai.com/reptile/) to a parallel version. I'm having 2 issues:

1. A TypeError at the call to `ray.init()` (`TypeError: save_reduce() got multiple values for argument 'obj'`)
2. If I ignore that TypeError by wrapping the call to `ray.init()` in a `try: ray.init();: except: pass` block, then I get to another error in execution. The training step of standard SGD (the `sgd` function) hangs forever, even with only a single model training at one time. 

### Source code / logs

Here's the full code:

```python3
#!/usr/bin/env python3
# -*- coding: utf-8 -*-

import operator
from collections import OrderedDict
from copy import deepcopy

import numpy as np
import ray
import torch
import torch.nn.functional as F
from torch import linspace, nn, sin
from torch.autograd import Variable
from torch.distributions import Number  # Somehow more robust than the std lib.
from torch.nn import Linear
from torch.optim import SGD
from torch.utils.data import DataLoader, TensorDataset


class ParamDict(OrderedDict):
    """A dictionary where the values are Tensors, meant to represent weights of
    a model.

    This subclass lets you perform arithmetic on weights.
    """

    def __init__(self, *args, **kwargs):
        super().__init__(*args, *kwargs)

    def _prototype(self, other, op):
        if isinstance(other, Number):
            return ParamDict({k: op(v, other) for k, v in self.items()})
        elif isinstance(other, dict):
            return ParamDict({k: op(self[k], other[k]) for k in self})
        else:
            return NotImplemented

    def __add__(self, other):
        return self._prototype(other, operator.add)

    def __rmul__(self, other):
        return self._prototype(other, operator.mul)

    __mul__ = __rmul__

    def __neg__(self):
        return ParamDict({k: -v for k, v in self.items()})

    def __rsub__(self, other):
        # a - b == a + (-b)
        return self.__add__(other.__neg__())

    __sub__ = __rsub__

    def __truediv__(self, other):
        return self._prototype(other, operator.truediv)


Weights = ParamDict
criterion = F.mse_loss

INPUT_SIZE, HIDDEN_SIZE, OUTPUT_SIZE = 1, 64, 1
N = 50  # Use 50 evenly spaced points on sine wave.

LR, META_LR = 0.02, 0.1  # Copy OpenAI's hyperparameters.
BATCH_SIZE, META_BATCH_SIZE = 1, 1
EPOCHS, META_EPOCHS = 1, 10
TEST_GRAD_STEPS = 2


def gen_task(num_pts=N) -> DataLoader:
    a = np.random.uniform(low=0.1, high=5)  # amplitude
    b = np.random.uniform(low=0, high=2 * np.pi)  # phase

    # Need to make `x` shape N,1 instead of N, to avoid
    # https://discuss.pytorch.org/t/dataloader-gives-double-instead-of-float
    x = linspace(-5, 5, num_pts)[:, None].float()
    y = a * sin(x + b).float()

    dataset = TensorDataset(x, y)

    loader = DataLoader(
        dataset,
        batch_size=BATCH_SIZE,
        shuffle=True,
        pin_memory=False,
    )

    return loader


class Model(nn.Module):
    def __init__(self, weights=None):
        super().__init__()
        self.fc1 = Linear(INPUT_SIZE, HIDDEN_SIZE)
        self.fc2 = Linear(HIDDEN_SIZE, HIDDEN_SIZE)
        self.out = Linear(HIDDEN_SIZE, OUTPUT_SIZE)

        # This has to be *after* the weight initializations or else we get a
        # KeyError.
        if weights is not None:
            self.load_state_dict(deepcopy(weights))

    def forward(self, x):
        x = F.relu(self.fc1(x))
        x = F.relu(self.fc2(x))
        return self.out(x)


# XXX hangs on this method
@ray.remote
def sgd(meta_weights: Weights, epochs: int) -> dict:
    """Run SGD on a randomly generated task."""

    model = Model(meta_weights)
    model.train()  # Ensure model is in train mode.

    task = gen_task()
    opt = SGD(model.parameters(), lr=LR)

    for epoch in range(epochs):
        for x, y in task:

            # XXX removing this section stops it hanging, but then it doesn't train
            x, y = Variable(x), Variable(y)
            loss = criterion(model(x), y)

            opt.zero_grad()
            loss.backward()
            opt.step()

    return model.state_dict()


def REPTILE(
    meta_weights,
    meta_batch_size: int = META_BATCH_SIZE,
    epochs: int = EPOCHS,
) -> Weights:
    """Run one iteration of REPTILE."""

    # XXX Hangs here
    weights = ray.get([sgd.remote(meta_weights, 1) for _ in range(meta_batch_size)])

    # The 0 * ParamDict(meta_weights) is because sum() needs a starting value.
    meta_weights += (META_LR / epochs) * sum((ParamDict(w) - meta_weights
                                              for w in weights), 0 * ParamDict(meta_weights))
    return meta_weights


if __name__ == '__main__':
    XXX TypeError here
    ray.init()

    meta_weights = Model().state_dict()

    for iteration in range(1, META_EPOCHS + 1):
        print(iteration)
        meta_weights = REPTILE(meta_weights)
```

issue_title: New changes are required for bootstrap4-alpha5 horizontal forms and grid layout.
issue_body:
New changes are necessary for Bootstrap4-alpha5 (some since alpha-3):
1.  `.form-control-label` was renamed to `.col-form-label` (since alpha-3)
Evidence and current markup for horizontal forms:
http://v4-alpha.getbootstrap.com/components/forms/#using-the-grid

2. `.form-group` is required to use together with `.row` again.
Evidence and current markup for horizontal forms:
http://v4-alpha.getbootstrap.com/components/forms/#using-the-grid
Related to #636 

3.  `.col-*-offset-*` are changed to `.offset-*-*`  (since alpha-3)
http://v4-alpha.getbootstrap.com/layout/grid/#example-offsetting-columns.
It would affect these recently made changes as well: https://github.com/django-crispy-forms/django-crispy-forms/pull/657/commits/832bc4bf1df87ebd15e2a09321c3e90cb5776fa3

4. `.form-control-feedback` is required for field error messages:
http://v4-alpha.getbootstrap.com/components/forms/#validation

Since I am familiar with changes I am willing to make a PR, if you're ready to accept changes for newest bootstrap4 alpha3-alpha5 changes. Users with bootstrap 4 < alpha3 will experience changes, but I think we must continue follow new bootstrap4 alphas markup.

issue_title: Baseline support
issue_body:
Once https://github.com/python-security/pyt/pull/100 is merged this will be do-able.

So a baseline is for when you want to diff between a previous run, (probably of known issues or false-positives) and a current run, 'as a big part of continuous integration', baseline support is super important.

See https://github.com/openstack/bandit as a tool that implements this.

```python
    parser.add_argument('-b', '--baseline',
                        help='path of a baseline report to compare against '
                             '(only JSON-formatted files are accepted)',
                        type=str,
                        default=None)
```

There is also the newly open sourced [`detect-secrets` repo](https://github.com/Yelp/detect-secrets/blob/b16acf1e8dc1e05366a9bfbd7ce35ed611adb94d/detect_secrets/core/baseline.py#L41) from the Yelp security team that implements this.

issue_title: It should be possible to get a traceback out of a cancel scope
issue_body:
With `fail_after`, it's a bit annoying that it can't show you where the block was cancelled. (The traceback just starts at the end of the scope.)

This will be a problem in particular for test timeouts. (Well, they probably won't use `fail_after`, but they'll have the same problem.)

I don't think we can capture the exceptions in cancel scope objects by default, because we don't want to pin the stacks in memory.

We could have a flag that tells it to save caught exceptions, though. Or try to do something clever with catching them ourselves inside the scope, but that's basically the same thing from the user point of view, and the cancel scope already knows how to reliably catch its own exceptions.

issue_title: Check for locked files before attempting pip upgrade of rlbot
issue_body:
If somebody has rocket league open and the dll injected, upgrading the rlbot python package will fail because the dll is locked. It would probably also fail if the interface dll was in use.

The batch files which attempt to upgrade rlbot should check first to see if the files are locked, and if so, skip the update. There are multiple examples of such batch files, one is here: https://github.com/RLBot/RLBotPythonExample/blob/master/run.bat#L5

Possible way to check for locked files: https://www.dostips.com/forum/viewtopic.php?t=5542

To locate the rlbot install location, consider using get_python_root() from here: https://github.com/RLBot/RLBot/blob/v4/src/main/python/rlbot/utils/class_importer.py#L86
